title: Coincya cintrana
intro: Coincya cintrana, also known as the Galician cabbage or Galician kale, is a species of plant in the family Brassicaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. The bioactive constituents of Coincya cintrana include glucosinolates, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Coincya cintrana is often used to make herbal teas and infusions, as well as being used in traditional Galician cuisine, where it is used to make soups and stews.
taxonomy: Coincya cintrana is classified within the Brassicaceae family of plants. This family includes various other plants such as cabbage, broccoli, and mustard. Coincya cintrana is a species of plant native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. The plant is thought to have evolved from a common ancestor with other Brassicaceae species.
